steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares pattern
steps.content: The expression inside the parentheses is $x^2 - 100y^2$. This fits the pattern $a^2 - b^2$ where $a = x$ and $b = 10y$ (since $(10y)^2 = 100y^2$).

steps.title: Apply the factorization formula
steps.content:
Substitute $a = x$ and $b = 10y$ into the difference of squares formula:
$$x^2 - 100y^2 = (x - 10y)(x + 10y)$$
Therefore:
$$17(x^2 - 100y^2) = 17(x - 10y)(x + 10y)$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
